mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Grafana instances and their resources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage GitOps workflows or manage Grafana resources as code. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator in the default namespace.

steps.title: Create a Grafana Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step involves creating a custom resource definition (CRD) for Grafana, which defines the desired state of your Grafana instance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> This command creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana Instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the details of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Removes the Grafana Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the Grafana instance and associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This step involves upgrading the Grafana Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backs up the current Grafana configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Grafana instance is running with the new version.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator using 'kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check the service type and ports.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The provided configuration for the Grafana instance may have syntax errors.

**Fix:**
Validate the YAML configuration and ensure all required fields are present.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana Operator not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana Operator pod may have crashed or failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator' and describe the pod for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' and the output of 'kubectl get grafana grafana' should show the instance details.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
